docs.title: Grimoire Documentation
docs.text:
Grimoire is a procedural content generation library for Windows x64 with a C-facing API.

This documentation is focused on practical integration guidance and curated API behavior details.

docs.title: What You Will Find
docs.text:
• Setup instructions for consuming headers and binaries
  • API guides for random and noise modules
  • Function-level signatures, constraints, and behavior notes
  • Practical recipes for terrain, clouds, and deterministic pipelines
  • Roadmap and platform expansion priorities
  • Notes on deterministic behavior and usage constraints
  • Security and disclosure policy links

docs.title: Suggested Reading Path
docs.text:
1. Start with Getting Started for integration and troubleshooting.
  2. Read API Overview and Common Types to understand ABI and seed conventions.
  3. Use Random API and Noise API for detailed behavior.
  4. Use Quick Reference for signature lookups.
  5. Use Recipes for real-world patterns.
  6. Check Roadmap for planned platform milestones.

docs.title: Project Status
docs.text:
Current release line: 1.0.x

For release history, see CHANGELOG.md in the repository root.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Windows x64 target.
  • A C, C++, or NASM-based consumer project.
  • Public headers from include/grimoire-pcg for C/C++ integration, or inc/grimoire_pcg.inc for NASM integration.
  • Built DLL plus matching import library from your build pipeline.

docs.title: Export Package Layout
docs.text:
The packaged export is organized as follows:

  • export/grimoire_pcg/bin/grimoire_pcg.dll
  • export/grimoire_pcg/bin/grimoire_pcg.lib
  • export/grimoire_pcg/inc/grimoire_pcg.inc
  • export/grimoire_pcg/include/grimoire-pcg/*.h

grimoire_pcg.lib is the import library used at link time on Windows. grimoire_pcg.dll must be present at runtime.

docs.title: C and C++ Integration Flow
docs.text:
1. Add export/grimoire_pcg/include to your compiler include paths.
  2. Link against export/grimoire_pcg/bin/grimoire_pcg.lib.
  3. Ensure export/grimoire_pcg/bin/grimoire_pcg.dll is available at runtime.
  4. Include API headers from grimoire-pcg/.

docs.title: NASM Integration Flow
docs.text:
1. Add export/grimoire_pcg/inc to NASM include paths.
  2. Link against export/grimoire_pcg/bin/grimoire_pcg.lib.
  3. Ensure export/grimoire_pcg/bin/grimoire_pcg.dll is available at runtime.
  4. Include grimoire_pcg.inc in assembly sources that call the library.

The exported .inc file declares the external procedure names for the public API so NASM modules can reference the DLL imports directly.

docs.title: Public Headers
docs.text:
• grimoire-pcg/common.h
  • grimoire-pcg/random.h
  • grimoire-pcg/noise.h

docs.title: NASM Import File
docs.text:
• grimoire_pcg.inc

The NASM import file exposes external declarations for:

  • RNG lifecycle and sampling functions
  • Serialization and cloning functions
  • Value noise, Perlin noise, FBM, and Billow entry points

docs.title: Minimal Deterministic RNG Example
docs.text:
#include <stdint.h>
#include <grimoire-pcg/random.h>

int main(void)
{
    GrimoireRandom rng = GrimoireRandom_CreateSeed(1337);
    if (!rng) {
        return 1;
    }

    int32_t roll = GrimoireRandom_NextRange(rng, 1, 6);
    (void)roll;

    GrimoireRandom_Destroy(rng);
    return 0;
}

docs.title: Minimal Noise Example
docs.text:
#include <grimoire-pcg/noise.h>

float sample(float x, float y)
{
    return Grimoire_Perlin2D(x, y, 0.0f, 2026);
}

docs.title: Minimal NASM Example
docs.text:
bits 64

%include "grimoire_pcg.inc"

section .data
    seed    dd 1337

section .text
global example_entry

example_entry:
    sub rsp, 40

    mov ecx, [rel seed]
    call GrimoireRandom_CreateSeed

    test rax, rax
    jz .done

    mov rbx, rax

    mov rcx, rax
    mov edx, 1
    mov r8d, 6
    call GrimoireRandom_NextRange

    mov rcx, rbx
    call GrimoireRandom_Destroy

.done:
    add rsp, 40
    ret

This example demonstrates the basic NASM flow:

  1. Include grimoire_pcg.inc.
  2. Call the exported creation function.
  3. Use the returned handle in subsequent API calls.
  4. Destroy the handle before returning.

When integrating from NASM, follow the Windows x64 calling convention expected by the DLL interface.

docs.title: Deterministic Content Pattern
docs.text:
For reproducible procedural content:

  1. Pick and document a fixed seed per world/map/profile.
  2. Keep call order stable across builds where exact replay matters.
  3. Persist RNG state when you need exact continuation points.

docs.title: Ownership and Lifetime Rules
docs.text:
• GrimoireRandom is opaque and heap-managed by the library.
  • Every successful create/clone/deserialize should be paired with destroy.
  • It is safe to call destroy with NULL.
  • The same ownership rules apply when handles are managed from NASM.

docs.title: Interop Notes (C++)
docs.text:
• Headers already provide extern "C" handling via macros.
  • Include the same headers directly in C++ without extra wrappers.

docs.title: Linker cannot resolve symbols
docs.text:
• Verify you linked the import library for the same build.
  • Confirm target architecture is x64.
  • For NASM projects, confirm the .obj output is linked with grimoire_pcg.lib.

docs.title: Runtime DLL not found
docs.text: • Put the DLL next to the executable or on an expected DLL search path.

docs.title: NASM include cannot be resolved
docs.text:
• Verify the assembler include path contains export/grimoire_pcg/inc.
  • Confirm the source uses %include "grimoire_pcg.inc".

docs.title: Non-reproducible outputs
docs.text:
• Ensure fixed seed usage.
  • Ensure identical function call order.
  • Avoid replacing deterministic create calls with non-deterministic create calls.

docs.title: Security Reminder
docs.text: GrimoireRandom is not cryptographically secure. Do not use it for cryptographic keys, secure tokens, or security boundaries.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text: This page summarizes the project roadmap for platform expansion. For the canonical version, see ROADMAP.md in the repository root.

docs.title: Current Direction
docs.text: Grimoire is currently focused on progressing from Windows-only support to broader cross-platform availability.

docs.title: Phase 1: Linux x64
docs.text:
Priority: highest

Planned outcomes:

  • Linux x64 build support for core library components
  • Repeatable toolchain and build flow
  • API compatibility validation against existing headers
  • Deterministic behavior checks aligned with current Windows expectations
  • Consumer documentation for Linux integration

docs.title: Phase 2: macOS
docs.text:
Priority: next, community-assisted

Planned outcomes:

  • macOS build bring-up
  • Platform validation for RNG and noise behavior parity
  • Documentation for macOS integration and runtime requirements

Community note:

  • macOS progress is expected to depend on contributor support and testing capacity.

docs.title: Phase 3: Beyond Initial Cross-Platform Support
docs.text: Direction after Linux and macOS stabilization is intentionally open. Future work may include performance optimization, developer tooling, and additional platform targets.

docs.title: How to Help
docs.text:
Contributions are especially helpful in these areas:

  • Platform build validation
  • CI pipeline setup and maintenance
  • Cross-platform behavior verification for deterministic APIs

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
For vulnerability reporting and support policy, see SECURITY.md at the repository root.

Key operational note:

  • GrimoireRandom is not cryptographically secure.
  • Do not use this library for cryptographic keys, secure tokens, or security-critical randomness.

docs.title: API Overview
docs.text: Grimoire exposes a C API through three public headers.

docs.title: Header Map
docs.text:
• common.h: shared macros, ABI export definitions, and base types
  • random.h: deterministic pseudo-random generator API
  • noise.h: value/perlin noise and fractal helpers

docs.title: Design Notes
docs.text:
• Stable C ABI surface through exported functions
  • Opaque random state handle to preserve implementation flexibility
  • Seed-driven deterministic behavior for reproducible procedural results

docs.title: Documentation Map
docs.text:
• Common Types: ABI macros, C/C++ linkage, and seed typing
  • Random API: lifecycle, sampling, state serialization, and cloning semantics
  • Noise API: base noise families, fractal composition, and tuning guidance
  • Quick Reference: compact signature index
  • Recipes: implementation patterns for real projects

docs.title: Return Value Expectations
docs.text:
• Most numeric APIs return scalar values directly.
  • Handle-creating APIs can return NULL and should always be checked.
  • Pointer-based inputs should be validated by consumers before calling.

docs.title: Determinism Contract
docs.text:
For deterministic workflows, reproducibility requires:

  • fixed seeds
  • consistent call order
  • consistent algorithm/function choice
  • consistent settings for fractal composition

docs.title: Common Types and Macros
docs.text: This page describes ABI-related macros and shared base types from common.h.

docs.title: Platform Normalization
docs.text:
common.h normalizes _WIN32 in some mixed toolchain environments:

#if !defined(_WIN32) && (defined(__WIN32__) || defined(WIN32) || defined(__MINGW32__))
    #define _WIN32
#endif

This helps keep export/import behavior consistent across Windows compilers.

docs.title: GRIMOIRE_API
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API controls symbol visibility.

Windows behavior:

  • If _GRIMOIRE_BUILD is defined: exports (__declspec(dllexport)).
  • Otherwise: imports (__declspec(dllimport)).

GCC/Clang behavior:

  • Uses __attribute__((visibility("default"))) when available.

Fallback:

  • If no platform rule applies, GRIMOIRE_API becomes empty.

docs.title: GRIMOIRE_DEPRECATED(msg)
docs.text:
Provides cross-compiler deprecation annotation:

  • MSVC: __declspec(deprecated(msg))
  • GCC/Clang: __attribute__((deprecated(msg)))
  • Fallback: empty macro

Use this for API evolution while preserving compatibility windows.

docs.title: C/C++ Linkage Macros
docs.text:
#ifdef __cplusplus
    #define GRIMOIRE_BEGIN extern "C" {
    #define GRIMOIRE_END }
#else
    #define GRIMOIRE_BEGIN
    #define GRIMOIRE_END
#endif

These macros wrap declarations so C++ consumers keep C symbol names.

docs.title: Base Seed Type
docs.text:
typedef int32_t hash_t;

hash_t is the shared deterministic seed type used across RNG and noise APIs.

Guidance:

  • Use constant seeds for reproducible tests and fixtures.
  • Document seed choices in gameplay/content pipelines.
  • Avoid using this type as a security token source.

docs.title: Consumer Build Checklist
docs.text:
• Ensure include path points to include/.
  • Define _GRIMOIRE_BUILD only when building the DLL itself.
  • Do not define _GRIMOIRE_BUILD in consuming applications.
  • In C++ code, include headers directly without wrapping additional extern "C" blocks.

docs.title: Noise API
docs.text: The noise module provides deterministic scalar noise functions for 1D, 2D, and 3D coordinates.

docs.title: Core Function Type
docs.text:
typedef float (*GrimoireNoiseFunction)(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);

This function type is used by fractal combiners to compose multiple octaves over a selected base noise.

docs.title: Coordinate Conventions
docs.text:
• 1D functions use x; y and z are ignored.
  • 2D functions use x and y; z is ignored.
  • 3D functions use all coordinates.
  • seed ensures deterministic output for identical input tuples.

docs.title: Sharp Value Noise
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSharp1D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSharp2D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSharp3D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);

Characteristics:

  • Abrupt transitions between cell values.
  • Useful for blocky, pixel-like, or stepped procedural patterns.
  • Output is documented as noise in approximately [0.0, 1.0].

docs.title: Smooth Value Noise
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSmooth1D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSmooth2D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSmooth3D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);

Characteristics:

  • Interpolated transitions between lattice values.
  • Better for soft gradients (height fields, texture masks, blend maps).
  • Output is documented as noise in approximately [0.0, 1.0].

docs.title: Perlin Noise
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Perlin1D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Perlin2D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Perlin3D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);

Characteristics:

  • Gradient-based smooth noise.
  • Natural-looking variation for terrain, clouds, and procedural motion.
  • Deterministic with fixed seed and coordinates.

docs.title: Fractal Combiners
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Fbm(
    GrimoireNoiseFunction noiseFunc,
    float x,
    float y,
    float z,
    hash_t seed,
    const GrimoireFractalSettings* settings);

GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Billow(
    GrimoireNoiseFunction noiseFunc,
    float x,
    float y,
    float z,
    hash_t seed,
    const GrimoireFractalSettings* settings);
  • Grimoire_Fbm: additive octave accumulation.
  • Grimoire_Billow: additive octave accumulation with per-octave absolute-value shaping for softer cloud-like ridges.

docs.title: GrimoireFractalSettings
docs.text:
typedef struct GrimoireFractalSettings
{
    float frequency;
    uint32_t octaves;
    float lacunarity;
    float persistence;
    bool staticSeed;
} GrimoireFractalSettings;

Parameter semantics:

  • frequency: starting sampling frequency for octave 0.
  • octaves: number of layers to sum.
  • lacunarity: frequency multiplier between octaves.
  • persistence: amplitude multiplier between octaves.
  • staticSeed: when true, uses a stable seed pattern across octaves.

Practical tuning ranges:

  • frequency: 0.005 to 0.05 for large-scale terrain-like features.
  • octaves: 3 to 8 for most game/runtime use.
  • lacunarity: around 2.0 as a baseline.
  • persistence: 0.35 to 0.65 for balanced roughness.

docs.title: Determinism Rules
docs.text:
Given the same:

  • function (ValueSharp, ValueSmooth, Perlin, Fbm, Billow)
  • coordinates
  • seed
  • fractal settings

the output is reproducible.

docs.title: Example: 2D Fractal Height Sample
docs.text:
#include <grimoire-pcg/noise.h>

float sample_height(float x, float y)
{
    GrimoireFractalSettings s;
    s.frequency = 0.02f;
    s.octaves = 5;
    s.lacunarity = 2.0f;
    s.persistence = 0.5f;
    s.staticSeed = true;

    return Grimoire_Fbm(Grimoire_Perlin2D, x, y, 0.0f, 1337, &s);
}

docs.title: Implementation Considerations
docs.text:
• Ensure settings points to a valid GrimoireFractalSettings instance.
  • In 1D and 2D variants, z is accepted for signature consistency but not used.
  • For higher octave counts, profile runtime cost against target performance budgets.
  • Treat outputs as deterministic procedural values, not cryptographic entropy.

docs.title: Random API
docs.text: The random module exposes an opaque deterministic pseudo-random number generator (PRNG).

docs.title: Type
docs.text:
typedef struct GrimoireRandom_t GrimoireRandom_t;
#define GrimoireRandom GrimoireRandom_t*
  • GrimoireRandom is a pointer handle.
  • The internal state layout is intentionally hidden.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_CreateNew
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API GrimoireRandom GrimoireRandom_CreateNew(void);

Creates a new instance with a non-deterministic seed source.

  • Returns: a valid GrimoireRandom or NULL on allocation/initialization failure.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_CreateSeed
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API GrimoireRandom GrimoireRandom_CreateSeed(hash_t seed);

Creates a deterministic instance.

  • Parameters: seed deterministic 32-bit seed (hash_t).
  • Returns: a valid GrimoireRandom or NULL on failure.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_Destroy
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API void GrimoireRandom_Destroy(GrimoireRandom random);

Releases resources owned by the instance.

  • Safe with NULL.
  • Must be called exactly once for each successful Create* result.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_Next
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API int32_t GrimoireRandom_Next(GrimoireRandom random);

Returns a pseudo-random signed 32-bit integer.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_NextRange
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API int32_t GrimoireRandom_NextRange(GrimoireRandom random, int32_t min, int32_t max);

Returns a pseudo-random integer in [min, max].

  • Precondition: min <= max.
  • Undefined behavior if min > max.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_NextMax
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API int32_t GrimoireRandom_NextMax(GrimoireRandom random, int32_t max);

Equivalent to GrimoireRandom_NextRange(random, 0, max).

  • Intended precondition: max >= 0.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_NextDouble
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API double GrimoireRandom_NextDouble(GrimoireRandom random);

Returns a uniform floating-point value in [0.0, 1.0).

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_NextBytes
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API void GrimoireRandom_NextBytes(GrimoireRandom random, void* buffer, size_t length);

Fills length bytes at buffer with pseudo-random data.

  • buffer must point to writable memory of at least length bytes.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_Serialize
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API void GrimoireRandom_Serialize(GrimoireRandom random, uint8_t* buffer);

Serializes current state into a caller-provided byte buffer.

  • Buffer size for this release: 228 bytes.
  • Serialized layout is implementation-defined.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_Deserialize
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API GrimoireRandom GrimoireRandom_Deserialize(const uint8_t* buffer);

Creates a new instance from serialized state.

  • Input buffer must come from GrimoireRandom_Serialize.
  • Returns: cloned state as new handle or NULL on failure.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_CloneInto
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API void GrimoireRandom_CloneInto(const GrimoireRandom source, GrimoireRandom destination);

Copies state from source into an existing destination instance.

docs.title: GrimoireRandom_Clone
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API GrimoireRandom GrimoireRandom_Clone(const GrimoireRandom source);

Allocates and returns a new state-identical clone.

docs.title: Determinism and Reproducibility
docs.text:
• Identical seed and call sequence produce identical outputs.
  • Cloned or deserialized states continue sequence from the same point.
  • Mixing deterministic and non-deterministic creation in one path will break reproducibility.

docs.title: Ownership and Safety Checklist
docs.text:
• Always check Create*, Clone, and Deserialize for NULL.
  • Destroy every created handle.
  • Validate range inputs before calling NextRange.
  • Keep serialized state buffers version-paired with this release.
  • Do not use for cryptographic purposes.

docs.title: Example: Save and Resume Sequence
docs.text:
#include <grimoire-pcg/random.h>

enum { GRIMOIRE_RANDOM_STATE_SIZE = 228 };

void sample_resume(void)
{
    uint8_t state[GRIMOIRE_RANDOM_STATE_SIZE];

    GrimoireRandom a = GrimoireRandom_CreateSeed(42);
    if (!a) return;

    (void)GrimoireRandom_Next(a);
    GrimoireRandom_Serialize(a, state);

    GrimoireRandom b = GrimoireRandom_Deserialize(state);
    if (b) {
        int32_t v1 = GrimoireRandom_Next(a);
        int32_t v2 = GrimoireRandom_Next(b);
        (void)v1;
        (void)v2;
        GrimoireRandom_Destroy(b);
    }

    GrimoireRandom_Destroy(a);
}

docs.title: Recipes
docs.text: This page shows practical usage patterns for common procedural workflows.

docs.title: Recipe: Reproducible World Seed
docs.text:
#include <grimoire-pcg/random.h>

typedef struct WorldSeedConfig {
    hash_t worldSeed;
} WorldSeedConfig;

int generate_spawn(WorldSeedConfig cfg)
{
    GrimoireRandom rng = GrimoireRandom_CreateSeed(cfg.worldSeed);
    if (!rng) return -1;

    int spawnIndex = GrimoireRandom_NextRange(rng, 0, 127);
    GrimoireRandom_Destroy(rng);
    return spawnIndex;
}

Use one stable seed per world profile to get reproducible generation.

docs.title: Recipe: Save/Load RNG Stream Position
docs.text:
#include <stdint.h>
#include <grimoire-pcg/random.h>

enum { RNG_STATE_SIZE = 228 };

void save_rng(GrimoireRandom rng, uint8_t outState[RNG_STATE_SIZE])
{
    GrimoireRandom_Serialize(rng, outState);
}

GrimoireRandom load_rng(const uint8_t inState[RNG_STATE_SIZE])
{
    return GrimoireRandom_Deserialize(inState);
}

Use this to continue procedural streams exactly after save/load.

docs.title: Recipe: Terrain-Like 2D FBM
docs.text:
#include <grimoire-pcg/noise.h>

float terrain_height(float x, float y, hash_t seed)
{
    GrimoireFractalSettings s;
    s.frequency = 0.01f;
    s.octaves = 6;
    s.lacunarity = 2.0f;
    s.persistence = 0.5f;
    s.staticSeed = true;

    return Grimoire_Fbm(Grimoire_Perlin2D, x, y, 0.0f, seed, &s);
}

Adjust frequency for world scale and octaves for detail density.

docs.title: Recipe: Soft Cloud Mask with Billow
docs.text:
#include <grimoire-pcg/noise.h>

float cloud_mask(float x, float y, hash_t seed)
{
    GrimoireFractalSettings s;
    s.frequency = 0.02f;
    s.octaves = 5;
    s.lacunarity = 2.0f;
    s.persistence = 0.45f;
    s.staticSeed = true;

    return Grimoire_Billow(Grimoire_Perlin2D, x, y, 0.0f, seed, &s);
}

Billow is useful when you want less directional detail and softer forms.

docs.title: Recipe: Byte Buffer Fill
docs.text:
#include <stdint.h>
#include <grimoire-pcg/random.h>

void fill_bytes(hash_t seed, uint8_t* buffer, size_t length)
{
    GrimoireRandom rng = GrimoireRandom_CreateSeed(seed);
    if (!rng) return;

    GrimoireRandom_NextBytes(rng, buffer, length);
    GrimoireRandom_Destroy(rng);
}

Use this for procedural data blobs, not security-sensitive entropy.

docs.title: API Quick Reference
docs.text: This page is a compact signature index for fast lookup.

docs.title: Random
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API GrimoireRandom GrimoireRandom_CreateNew(void);
GRIMOIRE_API GrimoireRandom GrimoireRandom_CreateSeed(hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API void GrimoireRandom_Destroy(GrimoireRandom random);
GRIMOIRE_API int32_t GrimoireRandom_Next(GrimoireRandom random);
GRIMOIRE_API int32_t GrimoireRandom_NextRange(GrimoireRandom random, int32_t min, int32_t max);
GRIMOIRE_API int32_t GrimoireRandom_NextMax(GrimoireRandom random, int32_t max);
GRIMOIRE_API double GrimoireRandom_NextDouble(GrimoireRandom random);
GRIMOIRE_API void GrimoireRandom_NextBytes(GrimoireRandom random, void* buffer, size_t length);
GRIMOIRE_API void GrimoireRandom_Serialize(GrimoireRandom random, uint8_t* buffer);
GRIMOIRE_API GrimoireRandom GrimoireRandom_Deserialize(const uint8_t* buffer);
GRIMOIRE_API void GrimoireRandom_CloneInto(const GrimoireRandom source, GrimoireRandom destination);
GRIMOIRE_API GrimoireRandom GrimoireRandom_Clone(const GrimoireRandom source);

docs.title: Noise
docs.text:
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSharp1D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSharp2D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSharp3D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSmooth1D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSmooth2D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_ValueSmooth3D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Perlin1D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Perlin2D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Perlin3D(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Fbm(GrimoireNoiseFunction noiseFunc, float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed,
                                const GrimoireFractalSettings* settings);
GRIMOIRE_API float Grimoire_Billow(GrimoireNoiseFunction noiseFunc, float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed,
                                   const GrimoireFractalSettings* settings);

docs.title: Fractal Settings
docs.text:
typedef struct GrimoireFractalSettings
{
    float frequency;
    uint32_t octaves;
    float lacunarity;
    float persistence;
    bool staticSeed;
} GrimoireFractalSettings;

docs.title: Noise Function Pointer Type
docs.text: typedef float (*GrimoireNoiseFunction)(float x, float y, float z, hash_t seed);
